title: rubus incisus subvar. koehneanus
intro: Rubus incisus subvar. koehneanus, also known as Koehne's cut-leaved bramble, is a subspecies of the Rubus incisus plant, which is a type of thorny shrub in the rose family. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi, making it a potential natural remedy for wound healing and infections. The health benefits of Rubus incisus subvar. koehneanus include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to fully understand its medicinal and health benefits.
